description_long: This hammer features a robust cylindrical metal head with two identical flat striking faces, suggesting it is a double-faced sledge or engineer's hammer. The head is solid and dark in color, with a smooth, slightly matte finish, and is securely mounted on a straight, natural wood handle. The handle displays visible wood grain patterns and is slightly contoured for grip, but lacks any additional ergonomic enhancements or rubberized grip. The overall construction appears sturdy and well-balanced, suitable for heavy-duty tasks such as breaking, striking, or driving objects. There are no visible brand markings, wear, or damage, and the hammer appears clean and unused. No attachments or accessories are present on the hammer.
description: A double-faced sledge hammer with a dark metal head and a straight, natural wood handle, showing no visible wear or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the cylindrical metal head with both striking faces visible.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the end of the handle and the symmetrical, double-faced head with both striking surfaces aligned.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, showing the other side of the handle and head.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the top of the handle and the back of the hammer head, with both striking faces visible.
attribute_handle_type: straight wooden handle with visible grain, slightly contoured, smooth surface, no additional grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: double-faced cylindrical sledge hammer head, both ends flat and suitable for striking, made of dark steel
